items.title: Stmnt: AI-powered bank statement analyzer
items.description: A Flutter iOS app that reads a bank statement PDF and turns it into spending analytics, forecasts, and recurring-subscription detection. The app runs a multi-stage AI pipeline on top of OpenRouter: extract, parse, normalize, convert currency, categorize, detect recurring charges, detect reversals, and remove duplicates (both at the file level using SHA-256 and at the transaction level). It uses 159 keyword rules across 17 categories, normalizes 100+ merchant brands, and supports 35+ currencies. Reports can be shared with other users through encrypted Supabase storage. Stmnt is live on the App Store and I ship updates to it every week.

items.title: On a Generalization of Kernel RLS to Nonlinear State-Space Systems
items.description: My senior research project at Khalifa University (May 2023). I derived the ARMA time-series learning algorithm from first principles, rewrote it as a least-squares problem in two unknowns, and turned it into the Kernel Recursive Least Squares (KRLS) algorithm in MATLAB. I then extended the construction to nonlinear state-space systems using tensor products in a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (the KAARMA algorithm). The project was supervised by Prof. Ibrahim M. Elfadel (EECS) and Prof. Jorge P. Zubelli (Mathematics).
items.chartCaption: Benchmarked on the Santa Fe laser time-series, the standard test for chaotic time-series prediction. NMSE (normalized mean squared error) measures how far a prediction lands from the true value. Lower is better.
